title: populus fremontii subsp. wislizeni
intro: Populus fremontii subsp. wislizeni, also known as the San Diego cottonwood, is a subspecies of the Fremont cottonwood tree native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The bark and leaves of this plant contain salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which gives it potential medicinal properties. The health benefits of Populus fremontii subsp. wislizeni include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce pain, fever, and swelling, as well as treat wounds and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat a variety of ailments, including menstrual cramps, colds, and sore muscles.
